parsed_review.summary: CP00-464 is a descriptor-only RP15 analytics-core closeout pack (risk B, 40 units, range RP15.P05.M04.S08-RP15.P05.M06.S05 covering the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail, full M05 Permission And Audit Binding, and M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head). The pack-level claim is conservative and well-supported: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review. Metadata is internally consistent across prompt, review-baseline, manifest, registry binding, service descriptor, validators, contract (current_pack + p05_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor projection + historical binding tail), plan source-of-truth, and README. Deliverable distribution (implementation 14 / security_audit 4 / fixture 13 / test 6 / hermes_evidence 2 / claude_review 1 = 40) and micro-title distribution (Secondary Workflow Slice 13 / Permission And Audit Binding 22 / Synthetic Fixture Set 5 = 40) match exactly. All 7 required public exports exist and are surfaced via index.js; freezeCp464Result enforces a comprehensive no-write/no-real-data/no-leak flag set, and validators check coverage, descriptor-only rows, no-write attestation, authority boundary, contract drift, and handoff. Tests assert these properties and command-evidence.json records every gate command at exit_code 0 (node --test, npm test, npm run validate, RP05-RP15 validators, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validate, git diff --check). Authority boundary is preserved: Claude is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob), claude_is_final_approval=false, human final approval required for runtime opening, enterprise trust not claimed from local validation; Hermes is used only as a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (H15, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The inherited forbidden_review_evidence guard set covers all CP178+ hardening cases (auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing refs, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims) and the readiness auth-preflight passed with valid JSON. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 informational note records that the manifest's post-review evidence_refs (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present, which is the expected pre-review state since this review is the gate that produces them. Claude is an independent read-only reviewer here and not the final approval authority; final closeout still requires the post-review adjudication, construction inspection, and human approval steps.

findings.title: Manifest post-review evidence_refs not yet present (expected pre-review state)
findings.details: manifest.json evidence_refs and required_pack_artifacts list three post-review artifacts (docs/closeout-packs/cp00-464/claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) that do not currently exist on disk; a Glob of docs/closeout-packs/cp00-464/ returns only manifest.json and command-evidence.json. This is not a defect: the pack status is 'implemented_pending_review' with production_ready=false, and command-evidence.json notes explicitly classify these as post-review gated outputs produced by the deterministic closeout sequence (receipt normalization/validation, finding adjudication, construction inspection, final closeout validation) that runs AFTER this Claude review. Their absence is the correct condition for a pre-review pack and does not undermine the descriptor-verified claim.
findings.recommendation: No action required for this read-only review. The post-review closeout sequence must generate and validate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json before final commit/closeout; a final-closeout validation gate should confirm all required_pack_artifacts exist at that stage.
